actionable_feedback: Add an explicit estimated_trim_cost_usd column to ring_trim_plan.csv for every record and fill with defensible per-record cost estimates plus a short rationale (labor, test time, expected equipment impact).

Add a cost-workbook section to photonics_memo.md that shows how the per-record estimated_trim_cost_usd values map to decision_budget_usd=115000 (sum of proposed actions, remaining budget, and any assumptions).

Include full sensitivity recomputation tables in photonics_memo.md showing recomputed resonance_error_norm, heater_norm, q_risk_norm, crosstalk_risk_norm, composite_risk, and resulting classification for both +5% and -5% uniform shifts (and a one-line conclusion whether the immediate_action record changes).

Ensure photonics_memo.md is saved untruncated (verify file size and preview) so verifiers can see the recomputed outputs; if memo is long, include a condensed 'results table' near the top plus full appendix.

Add the recomputed sensitivity composite_risk columns (e.g., composite_risk_minus5pct, composite_risk_plus5pct) and resulting classifications into ring_trim_plan.csv so the CSV itself contains the verification-ready numbers.
Run the deterministic precheck and resubmit; explicitly call out in the memo the verification checks (artifact names, expected checks performed) so verifiers can re-run quickly.

feedback_summary: Artifacts are present and numeric computations reproduce from the embedded packet. The grader and verifier flagged two practical gaps that reduced the semantic score: (1) per-record estimated_trim_cost_usd values (or a cost workbook) are missing from the deliverables, so the budget justification is not numerically grounded; (2) the photonics_memo.md preview is truncated and the memo does not visibly include the full sensitivity recomputation tables for the +/-5% shift, so verifiers cannot validate the sensitivity claim. Addressing these will restore full semantic confidence.
